authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(1) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Ellis Lake not listed)
CCR T14 §7.50 — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Ellis Lake not listed)
CCR T14 §7.50(a)(1) & §7.50(b)(152) — Lake Tahoe tributary special regulation (applies to tributary streams, not to lakes)
sources.quotedText: Every body of water listed in subsection (b) is closed to all fishing except during the open season as shown. (152) Tahoe Lake and tributaries (Placer and El Dorado Cos.). (A) Tahoe Lake tributaries upstream to the first lake. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout

sources.title: 2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (Updated 06/01/2026)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and 10 trout possession limit.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon — CDFW (Ellis Lake not listed; no CDFW plant planned)

parameters.note: Ellis Lake is a small alpine lake near Ellis Peak on the west rim of the Lake Tahoe basin (Placer Co., CA, Tahoe National Forest). It is NOT listed in CCR §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so the general statewide lake regulation (§5.85(a)(1)) governs — open all year. The Lake Tahoe tributary special regulation, §7.50(b)(152), applies only to Tahoe's tributary STREAMS 'upstream to the first lake', not to a lake; it does not apply here. Ellis Lake is absent from CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document, and no authoritative source documents current fish presence, so the species and stocking arrays are intentionally left empty (barren or undocumented).

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year (general statewide lake regulation; not listed as a special-regulation water). The §7.50(b)(152) Lake Tahoe tributary closure covers streams, not this lake.
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.counting_rule: General statewide trout limit for inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds: five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit, 10 trout possession limit
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
